# Spec context: §12.1 — Dialect differences

## Source

- Upstream: <https://abcnotation.com/wiki/abc:standard:v2.1#dialect_differences>
- Cached at: `crates/kaijutsu-abc/docs/abc-spec-cache.md` (line 2783)
- Section: 12. Dialects, strict / loose interpretation and backwards compatibility
- Subsection: 12.1 Dialect differences

Reproduced verbatim from the ABC v2.1 standard by Chris Walshaw,
under CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 — see `tests/fixtures/spec/README.md`.

## Preceding prose

> The main differences that have arisen are line-breaks, decoration delimiters and chord delimiters.
> By default, a (forced) [score line-break](#score_line-break_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") is typeset by using a [code line-break](#code_line-break_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") - see [typesetting line-breaks](#typesetting_line-breaks "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵").
> In the past the `!` symbol has instead been used to indicate [score line-breaks](#score_line-break_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") - this symbol is now used to denote decorations.
> *Comment:* The `!` symbol was introduced by abc2win, a very popular program in its time, although now moribund. Of the 160,000 tunes currently available in the [abcnotation.com tune search](https://abcnotation.com/search "https://abcnotation.com/search"), only around 1,600 (10%) use the ! symbol to 
> Although the use of the `!` symbol for line-breaking is now [deprecated](#outdated_syntax "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") (see [outdated line-breaking](#outdated_line-breaking "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵")), users who wish to continue using the `!` symbol for line-breaking merely need to include the "`I:linebreak !
> *Example:* The following abc code would result in two lines of music.
